title: Waiting
content:
I waited for the Master
        In the darkness dumb;
        Light came fast and faster--
        My light did not come!

        I waited all the daylight,
        All through noon's hot flame:
        In the evening's gray light,
        Lo, the Master came!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, George MacDonald delivers a powerful performance in "Waiting"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
